title: Fix it for now
summary: A woman brings her father's broken watch to an old clockmaker. He can fix it two ways. She has to choose.

body.speaker: Mai
body.text: Excuse me. Can you fix this?

body.speaker: Bác Thanh
body.text: Let me see. Ah. A watch. May I sit it on the cloth?

body.speaker: Bác Thanh
body.text: It is old. From the seventies, I would say.

body.speaker: Mai
body.text: My father's. He gave it to me when I was twelve.

body.speaker: Bác Thanh
body.text: So you have worn it for twenty-nine years.

body.speaker: Bác Thanh
body.text: And you have walked around with a broken watch for six months?

body.speaker: Mai
body.text: Because I knew you would ask difficult questions.

body.speaker: Bác Thanh
body.text: I would not have asked any. But now I will. Did your father stop wearing it because it broke, or did it break because he stopped wearing it?

body.speaker: Mai
body.text: He stopped wearing it because he died.

body.speaker: Mai
body.text: Six years ago. I have worn it since then. Six months ago it stopped. I think it was tired.

body.speaker: Bác Thanh
body.text: Watches do not get tired. But the people inside the watches sometimes do.

body.speaker: Bác Thanh
body.text: I can. It is a simple problem. A small part needs to be cleaned. I can do it in two days.

body.speaker: Bác Thanh
body.text: Do you want me to fix it so it runs forever, or so it runs for now?

body.speaker: Bác Thanh
body.text: If forever, I replace the small part with a new one. The watch is no longer fully your father's. If for now, I clean the old part. It will run for a year or two, then stop again.

annotations.meaningVi: Để tôi xem (thành ngữ xã giao khi muốn xem xét điều gì đó)
tôi đoán / theo tôi thấy (cách diễn đạt không chắc chắn, ý kiến mang tính dự đoán)
đi lại / di chuyển trong khi mang theo (cái gì đó)
những câu hỏi khó (collocation phổ biến)
không còn ... nữa (cấu trúc phủ định về trạng thái)
Thêm một câu hỏi nữa (cụm từ cố định để xin phép hỏi thêm)
Sự khác biệt là gì? (câu hỏi cố định khi cần giải thích sự khác nhau)
lúc này / tạm thời (chỉ khoảng thời gian hiện tại, không lâu dài)
Tôi đoán vậy / Tôi nghĩ vậy (biểu đạt rằng dự đoán của mình đã đúng)
Ông ấy đã tặng cho tôi (cấu trúc tặng gián tiếp: give + it + to + người)

grammarNotes.title: Present perfect with 'for' and 'since'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'have/has + V3' với 'for + khoảng thời gian' hoặc 'since + mốc thời gian'. Trong bài: 'You have worn it for twenty-nine years.' (Bạn đã đeo nó được hai mươi chín năm.) / 'I have worn it since then.' (Tôi đã đeo từ đó.) / 'You have walked around with a broken watch for six months?'
grammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'have/has + past participle' with 'for + period' or 'since + point'. From the dialogue: 'You have worn it for twenty-nine years.' / 'I have worn it since then.' / 'You have walked around with a broken watch for six months?'

cloze.template:
Mai: Excuse me. Can you fix this?
Bác Thanh: Let me see. Ah. A {{b1}}. May I sit it on the cloth?
Mai: Please.
Bác Thanh: It is old. From the {{b2}}, I would say.
Mai: My {{b3}}. He gave it to me when I was {{b4}}.
Bác Thanh: How old are you now?
Mai: {{b5}}.
Bác Thanh: So you have worn it for {{b6}} years.
Mai: Yes.
Bác Thanh: When did it stop?
Mai: Six months ago.
Bác Thanh: And you have walked around with a {{b7}} watch for six months?
Mai: Yes.
Bác Thanh: Why?
Mai: Because I knew you would ask difficult questions.
Bác Thanh: I would not have asked any. But now I will. Did your father stop wearing it because it broke, or did it break because he stopped wearing it?
Mai: He stopped wearing it because he {{b8}}.
Bác Thanh: Ah.
Mai: Six years ago. I have worn it since then. Six months ago it stopped. I think it was {{b9}}.
Bác Thanh: Watches do not get tired. But the people inside the watches sometimes do.
Mai: Can you fix it?
Bác Thanh: I can. It is a simple problem. A small part needs to be cleaned. I can do it in {{b10}} days.
Mai: Thank you.
Bác Thanh: One more question?
Mai: Yes.
Bác Thanh: Do you want me to fix it so it runs {{b11}}, or so it runs for now?
Mai: What is the difference?
Bác Thanh: If forever, I replace the small part with a new one. The watch is no longer fully your father's. If for now, I clean the old part. It will run for a year or two, then stop again.
Mai: {{b12}} the old part.
Bác Thanh: I thought so.

blanks.explanation: Bác Thanh identifies the object: 'Ah. A watch.'
He estimates the watch is from the seventies.
He gave it to her when she was twelve.
She is forty-one now — twenty-nine years after age twelve.
Twenty-nine years of wearing the watch.
She walked around with a broken watch for six months.
He stopped wearing it because he died — the painful answer Mai gives.
She thinks the watch was tired — Bác Thanh gently corrects this.
Two days to clean the part.
Run forever (replace the part) versus run for now (clean the old part).
Mai chooses: 'Clean the old part.' — she wants it to keep being her father's.

translationVi:
Mai: Xin chào. Bác sửa được cái này không?
Bác Thanh: Để bác xem. Ô. Một chiếc đồng hồ. Cho bác đặt nó lên miếng vải nhé?
Mai: Vâng ạ.
Bác Thanh: Cũ rồi. Từ thập niên bảy mươi thì phải.
Mai: Của bố cháu. Bố tặng cháu hồi cháu mười hai tuổi.
Bác Thanh: Giờ cháu bao nhiêu tuổi?
Mai: Bốn mươi mốt.
Bác Thanh: Vậy cháu đã đeo nó hai mươi chín năm.
Mai: Vâng.
Bác Thanh: Nó dừng khi nào?
Mai: Sáu tháng trước.
Bác Thanh: Và cháu đi lại với một chiếc đồng hồ hỏng suốt sáu tháng?
Mai: Vâng.
Bác Thanh: Sao thế?
Mai: Vì cháu biết bác sẽ hỏi những câu khó.
Bác Thanh: Bác sẽ không hỏi gì cả. Nhưng giờ bác sẽ hỏi. Bố cháu dừng đeo nó vì nó hỏng, hay nó hỏng vì bố cháu dừng đeo?
Mai: Bố cháu dừng đeo vì bố mất.
Bác Thanh: À.
Mai: Sáu năm trước. Từ đó cháu đeo. Sáu tháng trước nó dừng. Cháu nghĩ nó mệt.
Bác Thanh: Đồng hồ không biết mệt. Nhưng người bên trong đồng hồ thì đôi khi có.
Mai: Bác sửa được không ạ?
Bác Thanh: Sửa được. Vấn đề đơn giản. Một bộ phận nhỏ cần được lau sạch. Hai ngày là xong.
Mai: Cảm ơn bác.
Bác Thanh: Nhưng — bác hỏi thêm một câu nữa được không?
Mai: Vâng ạ.
Bác Thanh: Cháu muốn bác sửa để nó chạy mãi mãi, hay để nó chạy lúc này thôi?
Mai: Khác nhau thế nào ạ?
Bác Thanh: Nếu mãi mãi, bác thay bộ phận nhỏ đó bằng cái mới. Đồng hồ không còn hoàn toàn là của bố cháu nữa. Nếu chạy lúc này thôi, bác lau bộ phận cũ. Nó sẽ chạy một hai năm, rồi lại dừng.
Mai: Bác lau bộ phận cũ giúp cháu.
Bác Thanh: Bác nghĩ vậy.

questions.prompt: What does Mai bring to Bác Thanh?
options: Her father's old watch from the seventies
questions.hint: Look at the first few exchanges.

questions.prompt: How old was Mai when she received the watch?
questions.explanation: 'He gave it to me when I was twelve.'
questions.hint: Look at Mai's reply about her father.

questions.prompt: How long has Mai been wearing the watch?
questions.explanation: 'You have worn it for twenty-nine years.' — twelve to forty-one.

questions.hint: Look at Mai's reply about when it stopped.

questions.prompt: Why didn't Mai bring it sooner?
options: Because she knew he would ask difficult questions
She did not know where the shop was
questions.explanation: 'Because I knew you would ask difficult questions.'

questions.prompt: Why did Mai's father stop wearing the watch?
questions.explanation: 'He stopped wearing it because he died.'
questions.hint: Look at Mai's answer to Bác Thanh's harder question.

questions.prompt: What does Bác Thanh say about Mai's idea that the watch was 'tired'?
options: He gently disagrees: watches do not get tired; but the people inside the watches sometimes do
He says the watch is dead
questions.explanation: 'Watches do not get tired. But the people inside the watches sometimes do.'
questions.hint: Look at Bác Thanh's reply after Mai says 'tired'.

questions.prompt: What are the two options he offers?
options: Fix it so it runs forever (replace the small part) — or so it runs for now (clean the old part)
Fix it today or next week
questions.explanation: Detailed in Bác Thanh's two-part question.

questions.prompt: What is the trade-off Bác Thanh explains?
options: Forever is cheap; for now is expensive
Forever replaces the part — the watch is no longer fully her father's; for now keeps the old part but the watch will stop again in a year or two
questions.explanation: 'If forever, I replace the small part with a new one. The watch is no longer fully your father's.'
questions.hint: Look at Bác Thanh's long answer.

questions.prompt: Which option does Mai choose, and what is Bác Thanh's reaction?
options: Clean the old part; he says 'I thought so'
questions.hint: Look at the last two turns.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Mai chooses to keep her father's part, even knowing the watch will stop again. Think of something in your life that you could 'fix forever' by replacing — but choose not to. What does keeping the original cost you, and what does it give you?
